tokens: (Pause) And we have a beautiful day planned for the two of you.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Joking) Has the Sun risen over Hadley Mountain, yet?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Well, give us about 30 minutes here and we'll take a look.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I wouldn't be at all surprised and I've got things for you to copy.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: When we repressed the cabin after yesterday's EVA, my (PLSS) water valve was not completely off, and we lost a little water out of the sublimator during the repress.
Then during the recharge, I noticed on the Aux water vent portion that initially there were a lot more bubbles in the sight glass.
After the 10 seconds, why, it got down to a point there were just a very few bubbles.
But I'm wondering whether there might not be a special procedure involved because we did the repress with the water valve open.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We'll think about it and be back with you with an answer in a few minutes.
By the way, the passive seismometer people tell me that there must be somebody moving around in the LM.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Oh, we're moving this morning, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And, Jim, I have updates to read to you when you're comfortably ready.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (As per Surface 8-9) I'll start with lift-off times.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Joking) Contact tower when you're ready for departure.
And I have LM consumables update when you're ready for those.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: At GET (Ground Elapsed Time) 160: RCS A, 85.0; B, 85.0; O2 descent 1, 59.9; 2, 56.7; O2 ascent number 1, 99; number 2, 99; H2O descent number 1, 18.3; number 2, 16.5; H2O ascent, 100 percent on both 1 and 2; amp-hours: descent, 803; ascent, 572.
And I have a note on your descent water when you're ready.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Just wanted you to be aware that the descent water (gauge) may show, over the next few hours, between 1 and 0 pounds.
You'll have actual usable water, perhaps as much as twenty-two-and-a-half pounds.
It might not be quite that much.
It depends upon the measurement errors.
However, we're very well aware of your water situation; it's no problem at all.
We just don't want you to be alarmed if you come up with a zero reading at any time on that water.
And, that's about all we have for the time being.
We're standing by for your crew status report.
We'd like also the radiation devices this morning.
And I think we have a very nice traverse plan laid out for you.
We can talk about that as you start to get ready.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: And, Dave and Jim, basically the EVA is going to last somewhere between 4 and 5 hours (depending on how quickly they get outside), so it will be a short EVA.
I'm told that we checked off the 100-percent science-completion square sometime during EVA-1 or maybe even shortly into EVA-2.
From here on out, it's gravy all the way; and we're just going to play it cool, take it easy, and see some interesting geology.
It should be a most enjoyable day.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Accidental mike key) Re-do my sensors here.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Accidental mike key) They probably want us to hit the rille for all that good rille photography.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: You're keying your mike off and on.
You might be sitting on it or stepping on it or pressing on it.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I have Biomed, Left, right now.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I don't know if you copied.
I just wanted you to be aware your mike was being keyed on from time to time, perhaps inadvertently.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Regarding your PLSS question, everything looks normal to us down here.
It seems like there's no problem there at all.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: And, Joe, Dave fixed my camera last night, so there shouldn't be any problem with it this morning.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Joking) And I bet he fixed it with a piece of tape, didn't he?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I'm sensored now, and I'm going to put the Biomed on Right so you can check on my sensors.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We're about to be off comm here for a short period.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We copy and we're looking for Biomed, Left, when you do.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Our inventory shows that we do not have any more color Mags available.
Can you check your inventory down there?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Long Pause) Hadley Base, this is Houston.
We think, Jim, that Mag Tango Tango is on your camera in the ETB now, and Tango Tango is color.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: We do have color on my camera.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We're looking for a rough hack on where you might be in your EVA number 3 Prep.
We're standing by for a crew status report plus the radiation devices (as per Surface 8-8).
And, we're wondering when you might be switching over for Dave's biomed data.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: He's getting in his suit now.
We could switch over to his biomed right now.
Well, stand by, it'll be a few minutes.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Why don't you check Dave's biomed now.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Do you want to leave the Biomed on Left?
And I'm going S-band voice to voice.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And, are you ready for the battery management called out at 160(:15)?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We'll be starting the EVA-3 prep card in about 3 or 4 minutes.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We copy that, and we want to just remind you again on the bottom of 8-10, the page in your (Surface) checklist there, the
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: We're still showing the Cabin Gas Return in Cabin.
Could you reverify that it's in Egress, please.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Cabin Gas Return is presently in Auto.
I think we pick it up at Egress a little later on in the procedure.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Actually we should have picked that up on page 8-10 in your checklist.
So we think it should be in the Egress position now.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: We'll be coming to you with a comm check (on Surface 9-3) in about 2 or 3 minutes.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave and Jim, this is Houston.
Be advised we've got an EVA update for you, which you can copy onto the checklist if you want, or we can read it to you as you progress through the EVA, and we can talk about that when you want to.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Well, where does it fall, Joe, and what does it concern.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: It's going to be very simple changes to the checklist.
I think just a few words concerning the general picture will be plenty sufficient; and then we'll give you the details as you come to them.
I just want to give you an idea of how the traverse is going to look before you actually get started.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: You're going to be able to follow your cuff checklist for EVA-3 almost exactly.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Well, can we proceed on here and you want to give us the changes now?
It might be better for us to proceed on out, and you change us as we drive or something.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: That sounds like the second option is a good one, Dave.
Although you might want the big picture before you proceed on out.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Well, why don't you give us the big picture now, before we get too deep in the comm checks and all.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Taking it from the top, we're going to ask you to stop first at the ALSEP site and spend a few minutes recovering the successfully drilled core tube and, then follow that with the Grand Prix photography.
From there, press on towards Station 9, as planned.
We're going to skip the Delta stop in between.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Station 9 is exactly as we planned it.
up to Station 10, exactly as we planned it; and at Station 10, we're going to hit a branch point.
We can update you there when you arrive at Station 10.

The two options are basically, to head north for the Complex, although we think it's more probable we'll just want to loop back towards the north across Alligator Chain doing good mare sampling, and wind up at Quark West crater, that's the western crater of the Quark triplet, and use that as a Station 14 stop.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I guess we'll proceed on to Station 10 and take a look at it there.
I'd sort of would like to get up to the North Complex if we can.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We copy that, and it may well be we can get up there.
We'll just see how it goes.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Reading from the last lines of Surface 9-3) "Mode, Vox; Vox Sensitivity, Max; VHF A, T/R; B, Receive." (Reading from Surface 9-4) Okay, "CB(16) Comm: SE Audio, open; and collect (sic)"...Have you connected those...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, "CB(16) Comm: SE Audio, closed; PLSS PTT, Maintain, Right; PLSS mode to A; wheel, counter-clockwise."

tokens: Okay, you're 5 square (that is, 5 by 5) to me; check with Houston.

tokens: Okay, Houston, how do you read the CDR on A?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Tone on." (Pause) Okay, how do you read me on AR?

tokens: Joe, how do you read the LMP?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, Jim, you're 5 by and the TM's good.

tokens: "CB(16) ECS: LCG Pump, closed, which it is; cold as required.
CB(16) ECS: Cabin Repress, close; verify."
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, "verify ECS caution, H2O Sep Component lights." About a minute.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Why don't I take care of you and you take care of me?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, you want me to put you on Suit Disconnect?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Why don't you come on around?
(Long Pause) Okay, you're on Suit Disconnect, yeah.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Secure that about the PGA, Dave.
Why don't you put those under one of my belts?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) Okay, "OPS O2 hose is closed and locked The purge valve"...Okay, it's closed.
Locked low and the pin's in.
Get your PGA diverter valve to vertical.
(Pause) Okay, and you can get me.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, we'll go to Suit Disconnect on you.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Low (Pause) Okay, it's connected and locked.
Okay, and your PGA diverter valve should be vertical.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, we should drop that back as far as we possibly can.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: That's going to come out with the ETB.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: PLSS Fan to On, to the right.
And vent flags should clear." Mine clears.

tokens: "Secure tool harness self-doff straps to LEVAs." Okay, let me get yours.

tokens: Let's see if you can get back in your corner.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Connect the PLSS water hose." Okay, in work.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Says, "Connect PLSS ...Okay, stow LM hoses." (Long Pause) I'll have to wait until you turn around here.

tokens: Okay, they're aligned and adjusted and locked.

That one's locked and the bootie's on.

tokens: Let me get the bootie around...(Pause) Got booties on all those connectors.

Cuff gauge should come up 3.7 to 4.0." (Pause) And I'm coming up.
